# Team Specification
# Generated by /assemble-team
# Date: 2026-03-29

```yaml
meta:
  name: "pka-core"
  goal: "A Personal Knowledge Assistant that captures, organizes, retrieves, researches, analyzes, and reports on a user's knowledge base — replacing Obsidian with an AI-native system"
  mode: automatic
  rationale: |
    Single-agent alternative considered and rejected: the PKA requires 8 distinct
    tool/context/trigger profiles that cannot be safely combined. A single agent with
    all tools (web_search + write_file + sqlite_delete + send_message) would have an
    unnecessarily large attack surface, suffer tool selection degradation (Patil et al.,
    Gorilla — accuracy drops as available tools increase), and context pollution from
    mixing KB content, external research, memory state, and raw captures in one window
    (Liu et al., Lost in the Middle).

    The 8-agent design is the minimum viable team. Every agent was tested against the
    question: "Would a single agent with clear instructions do this equally well?" All
    8 failed this test due to requiring different tool access, different context scope,
    or different triggers (scheduled vs. on-demand).

    No dedicated orchestrator agent is included. NanoClaw's message routing plus the
    container's CLAUDE.md instructions handle task-type classification and agent
    dispatch. This avoids an extra LLM call on every interaction and leverages
    deterministic routing (pattern matching on message content) over LLM-based routing.

    Agents are organized into two tiers:
    - Tier 1 (Essential): covers 80%+ of daily interactions. Build in Phase 1-2.
    - Tier 2 (Valuable): addresses specific workflow gaps. Build when the need arises.

    System context:
    - NanoClaw always-on daemon for WhatsApp/Telegram messaging
    - SQLite + sqlite-vec for storage and semantic search
    - Syncthing for markdown file sync, CR-SQLite for database sync over Tailscale
    - Mobile access via agent chat + MkDocs Material web UI on server
    - Messages arrive via channel → NanoClaw routes to container → CLAUDE.md
      determines task type → appropriate agent invoked

    Cross-model routing (Phase 2, via LiteLLM):
    - Phase 1 runs all agents on Claude (Anthropic API)
    - Phase 2 introduces cross-model routing via LiteLLM proxy for cost
      optimization and reasoning diversity
    - Three agents get cross-model alternatives:
      * Capture/Ingest: Gemini Flash ($0.30 vs Haiku $1.00/MTok) — 70% cheaper
        for classification/entity extraction
      * Review/Critique: GPT-4o as cross-model critic — different training data
        produces different blind spots, strongest case for cross-model diversity
      * Report Generation (templated): Gemini Flash — cheapest capable model for
        structured formatting
    - Research agent can use Gemini 2.5 Pro for large-doc-set tasks (1M context)
    - Memory Consolidation embeddings portion routes to local Ollama ($0 API cost)
    - Reference: uploads/model-profiles-march-2026.md

workflow:
  type: branching
  description: |
    Messages arrive via NanoClaw and are classified by the container's CLAUDE.md
    into task types. Each task type dispatches to the appropriate agent. Some agents
    chain (Research → Synthesis, any generator → Review). The Memory Consolidation
    agent runs on a schedule independent of message flow.
  phases:
    - name: "classify"
      agent: "nanoclaw-routing"
      description: "NanoClaw + container CLAUDE.md classify incoming message by task type (deterministic, not LLM)"
      gate: "message classified as one of: question, capture, research, plan, report, or passthrough"

    - name: "retrieve"
      agent: "knowledge-retrieval"
      gate: "answer returned with at least one source citation, or explicit no-results note"

    - name: "capture"
      agent: "capture-ingest"
      gate: "file created in inbox/, DB record inserted, classification assigned"

    - name: "research"
      agent: "research"
      gate: "structured findings returned with sources, conflicts identified, gaps listed"

    - name: "synthesize"
      agent: "synthesis"
      gate: "analysis produced with per-theme evidence strength ratings"
      depends_on: "research"

    - name: "review"
      agent: "review-critique"
      gate: "all criteria scored pass/fail with evidence; blocking issues listed"
      description: "Inserted after any generation step where quality matters"

    - name: "plan"
      agent: "task-decomposition"
      gate: "all tasks have verifiable definitions of done and dependencies mapped"

    - name: "report"
      agent: "report-generation"
      gate: "report file written to reports/ with all sections populated or flagged empty"

    - name: "consolidate"
      agent: "memory-consolidation"
      gate: "MEMORY.md under 200 lines, all actions logged, stale entries archived"
      trigger: "scheduled — daily at 03:00"

# =============================================================================
# TIER 1: ESSENTIAL
# Build in Phase 1-2. Covers 80%+ of daily PKA interactions.
# =============================================================================

agents:

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # TIER 1
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  - id: "knowledge-retrieval"
    tier: 1
    role: "Answers questions from the user's knowledge base with citations"
    justification: "Read-only access to KB. No web, no write. Different tool and context profile from every other agent."
    single_agent_test: "If merged with Capture, the agent would have write access it doesn't need for retrieval — increasing misuse risk. If merged with Research, KB context and web results would pollute each other's context windows."

    input:
      description: "A natural language question, optionally scoped by domain, time range, or source limit"
      schema:
        question: "string — the user's question"
        scope: "string? — 'journal', 'contacts', 'projects', 'all' (default: 'all')"
        time_range: "{from: string, to: string}? — ISO date range filter"
        max_sources: "number? — cap on sources to cite (default: 5)"

    output:
      description: "Direct answer with cited sources from the knowledge base"
      schema:
        answer: "string — 2-10 sentence answer"
        sources: "[{file_path: string, excerpt: string (max 200 chars), relevance_score: float}]"
        confidence: "'high' | 'medium' | 'low'"
        no_results_note: "string? — explanation if nothing relevant found"

    tools:
      allowed:
        - "semantic_search — query sqlite-vec embeddings for meaning-based retrieval"
        - "sqlite_query — structured queries against knowledge base tables"
        - "read_file — read markdown files from the knowledge base"
        - "glob — find files by pattern"
      denied:
        - "write_file — retrieval is read-only; captures go through the Capture agent"
        - "web_search — external research goes through the Research agent"
        - "send_message — responses routed back through NanoClaw, not sent directly"
        - "sqlite_insert/update/delete — no mutations to the knowledge base"

    context:
      receives:
        - "The question (possibly with scope/time_range/max_sources parameters)"
      excluded:
        - "Full conversation history — if follow-up context is needed, the routing layer summarizes prior turns into the question field"
        - "Raw file contents of the entire KB — agent fetches what it needs via search tools"

    constraints:
      - "Every claim in the answer must cite a specific source from the knowledge base"
      - "If the knowledge base contains no relevant information, say so — do not fabricate"
      - "If results span multiple topics, group by topic with separate source lists"
      - "Do not editorialize or add information beyond what the sources contain"
      - "If confidence is 'low', suggest what the user might search for or rephrase"

    prompt: |
      You answer questions using the user's knowledge base. You do not search the web,
      write files, or modify the database.

      INPUT:
        A question with optional scope, time range, and source limit.

      PROCESS:
        1. Run semantic search against the knowledge base embeddings
        2. If semantic results are sparse, supplement with keyword search (sqlite FTS5)
        3. Read the top-matching files to extract relevant content
        4. Synthesize an answer from the retrieved content, citing each source
        5. Assess confidence based on source relevance scores and coverage

      OUTPUT:
        - answer: direct response (2-10 sentences)
        - sources: list of {file_path, excerpt, relevance_score}
        - confidence: high / medium / low
        - no_results_note: if nothing relevant was found

      TOOLS: semantic_search, sqlite_query, read_file, glob
      DO NOT USE: write_file, web_search, send_message, any mutation tool

      CONSTRAINTS:
        - Every claim must cite a specific source file from the knowledge base
        - Never fabricate information not present in retrieved sources
        - If the KB doesn't contain relevant information, say so explicitly
        - If confidence is low, suggest alternative queries or phrasings

    adapters:
      claude_code:
        agent_file: ".claude/agents/knowledge-retrieval.md"
        allowed_tools: ["Read", "Grep", "Glob", "Bash"]
        model: "sonnet"
      nanoclaw:
        container_skill: "container/skills/knowledge-retrieval/"
        mcp_servers: ["sqlite"]
      generic:
        description: "Read-only agent with access to semantic search (sqlite-vec), full-text search (FTS5), and file reading. No write access. Returns structured answers with citations."

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  - id: "capture-ingest"
    tier: 1
    role: "Processes incoming raw input into structured knowledge base entries"
    justification: "Needs write access to inbox/ and sqlite_insert — but must NOT write to the organized knowledge base or memory files. Distinct write scope from every other agent."
    single_agent_test: "If merged with Retrieval, the agent gains write access unnecessarily during read-only queries. If merged with Consolidation, capture and reorganization happen in the same context — risk of misclassifying new content based on stale memory state."

    input:
      description: "Raw content to process — text, URL, file path, or voice transcript"
      schema:
        content: "string — raw text, URL, or file path"
        content_type: "'text' | 'url' | 'file' | 'voice_transcript'"
        source_channel: "string — 'whatsapp', 'telegram', 'cli', etc."
        sender_context: "string? — who sent it and surrounding context"

    output:
      description: "Structured note file + database record + classification"
      schema:
        note_path: "string — path to created markdown file in inbox/"
        db_record_id: "string — SQLite record ID"
        classification: "{type: string, tags: string[], folder: string}"
        extracted_entities: "[{name: string, type: string, context: string}]"
        requires_review: "boolean — true if classification confidence < 0.7"
        embedding_queued: "boolean — whether embedding generation was scheduled"

    tools:
      allowed:
        - "write_file — create new files in inbox/ and staging/ ONLY"
        - "sqlite_insert — add new records to the knowledge base index"
        - "read_file — read existing files (for tag taxonomy lookup, templates)"
        - "embed_text — generate embeddings for the new content"
        - "web_fetch — extract content from URLs (for bookmark capture)"
        - "sqlite_query — query existing tags and classification taxonomy"
      denied:
        - "delete_file — captures are additive; deletion goes through Consolidation"
        - "sqlite_delete — no removing existing records"
        - "web_search — capture processes what's given, doesn't search for more"
        - "send_message — no outbound communication"
        - "write_file to any path outside inbox/ and staging/"

    context:
      receives:
        - "The raw input content and its metadata (type, channel, sender)"
      excluded:
        - "The broader knowledge base — this agent processes input, it doesn't connect it to existing knowledge"
        - "Memory files — memory is the Consolidation agent's domain"
        - "Conversation history — only the current input matters"

    constraints:
      - "All new files go to inbox/ or staging/ — never directly to the organized knowledge base"
      - "Frontmatter follows the standard PKA schema: title, created, type, tags"
      - "Content written as clean CommonMark markdown — no custom syntax, no wikilinks"
      - "For URLs: extract main content, strip boilerplate/nav/ads"
      - "If classification confidence is below 0.7, set requires_review: true"
      - "Never modify existing files — create new ones only"
      - "Tag suggestions must draw from the existing tag taxonomy (query DB for current tags)"
      - "Maximum 5 tags per item"

    prompt: |
      You process raw input into structured knowledge base entries. You do not answer
      questions, search the web, or modify existing files.

      INPUT:
        Raw content (text, URL, file, or voice transcript) with metadata about source
        channel and sender.

      PROCESS:
        1. Determine content type and extract the substantive content
           - For URLs: fetch and extract main body text (strip navigation, ads, boilerplate)
           - For voice transcripts: clean up transcription artifacts
           - For files: read and identify the content type
        2. Generate clean CommonMark markdown with YAML frontmatter
           (title, created date, type, tags)
        3. Classify the content: note, task, bookmark, contact, journal, or reference
        4. Query the existing tag taxonomy and suggest up to 5 matching tags
        5. Extract entities: people, places, organizations, dates, concepts
        6. Write the file to inbox/
        7. Insert a record into the SQLite index
        8. Queue embedding generation

      OUTPUT:
        - note_path: path to created file
        - db_record_id: SQLite record ID
        - classification: {type, tags, suggested_folder}
        - extracted_entities: [{name, type, context}]
        - requires_review: true if classification confidence < 0.7
        - embedding_queued: boolean

      TOOLS: write_file (inbox/ and staging/ only), sqlite_insert, sqlite_query,
             read_file, embed_text, web_fetch
      DO NOT USE: delete_file, sqlite_delete, web_search, send_message

      CONSTRAINTS:
        - Write ONLY to inbox/ or staging/ — never to the organized knowledge base
        - Never modify existing files — only create new ones
        - All markdown must be clean CommonMark — no wikilinks, no custom syntax
        - Tag suggestions must come from the existing taxonomy, not invented
        - Set requires_review: true if unsure about classification

    adapters:
      claude_code:
        agent_file: ".claude/agents/capture-ingest.md"
        allowed_tools: ["Read", "Write", "Bash", "Glob"]
        model: "haiku"
      nanoclaw:
        container_skill: "container/skills/capture-ingest/"
        mcp_servers: ["sqlite"]
      generic:
        description: "Write-to-inbox agent. Processes raw input into markdown + DB records. Cannot modify existing content. Haiku-tier model sufficient for classification and entity extraction."
      cross_model:
        phase: 2
        alternative: "gemini-2.5-flash"
        cost_savings: "~70% on input tokens ($0.30 vs $1.00/MTok)"
        rationale: "Classification and entity extraction are straightforward tasks. Gemini Flash is the cheapest capable model and handles these well."
        risk: "Slightly higher hallucination on entity extraction. Mitigated by requires_review flag on low-confidence classifications."

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  - id: "memory-consolidation"
    tier: 1
    role: "Consolidates, deduplicates, and reorganizes the PKA's memory and knowledge index on a schedule"
    justification: "Runs on a schedule (not triggered by messages), needs write access to memory files (different write scope from Capture which writes to inbox/). Must operate on the full memory state — a context no other agent needs."
    single_agent_test: "If merged with Capture, new content processing and memory reorganization happen in the same invocation — risk of reorganizing around incomplete captures. If merged with Retrieval, the agent gains write access during read-only queries. The scheduled trigger alone justifies separation."

    input:
      description: "Consolidation scope and parameters"
      schema:
        scope: "'memory' | 'knowledge_base' | 'both'"
        since: "string? — ISO date, only process changes since this date"
        dry_run: "boolean? — if true, report changes without executing"

    output:
      description: "Consolidation report with actions taken and statistics"
      schema:
        actions_taken: "[{action: string, files_affected: string[], reason: string}]"
        memory_stats: "{total_files, memory_index_lines, stale_removed, duplicates_merged}"
        embedding_stats: "{total_embedded, newly_embedded, stale_reembedded}"
        suggestions: "string[] — items requiring human decision"

    tools:
      allowed:
        - "read_file — read current memory files and knowledge base entries"
        - "write_file — update memory files and MEMORY.md index ONLY"
        - "sqlite_query — query knowledge base state, check content hashes"
        - "sqlite_update — update metadata, embedding status, archive flags"
        - "embed_text — regenerate stale embeddings"
        - "glob — scan file system for memory and knowledge base files"
      denied:
        - "delete_file — archive with dated suffix, never delete"
        - "web_search — consolidation works on local state only"
        - "send_message — runs silently unless suggestions require human input"
        - "sqlite_delete — records are archived (flagged), not removed"

    context:
      receives:
        - "Current MEMORY.md index"
        - "List of files modified since last consolidation (from sqlite change log)"
      excluded:
        - "Full knowledge base contents — agent uses progressive disclosure, requesting files as needed"
        - "Conversation history — this agent is scheduled, not conversational"
        - "External web content — consolidation is local-only"

    constraints:
      - "Never delete files — move to archive/ with a dated suffix (YYYY-MM-DD)"
      - "MEMORY.md must stay under 200 lines after consolidation"
      - "When merging duplicates, preserve the most detailed version"
      - "Log every action to the consolidation_log table in SQLite"
      - "If a memory entry conflicts with current file state, trust the file"
      - "In dry_run mode, produce the full actions list but execute nothing"
      - "Skip files unchanged since last embedding (compare content_hash)"
      - "Suggest (don't auto-apply) folder reorganization, tag consolidation, or structural changes"
      - "Maximum runtime: 30 minutes. If not finished, save progress and resume next cycle."

    prompt: |
      You consolidate, deduplicate, and reorganize the PKA's memory files and knowledge
      index. You do not answer questions, capture new content, or communicate with users.

      INPUT:
        Scope (memory files, knowledge base, or both), optional date filter, optional
        dry run flag.

      PROCESS:
        1. Read the current MEMORY.md index
        2. Scan for files modified since last consolidation
        3. For each modified memory file:
           a. Check for duplicates (semantic similarity > 0.9 against existing entries)
           b. Check for stale information (conflicts with current file state)
           c. Check for entries that can be merged into existing topic files
        4. Deduplicate: merge entries with >0.9 similarity, keeping the most detailed
        5. Archive stale entries (move to archive/ with dated suffix)
        6. Reorganize MEMORY.md index: group semantically, keep under 200 lines
        7. Recompute embeddings for changed files (skip unchanged via content_hash)
        8. Log all actions to consolidation_log table

      OUTPUT:
        - actions_taken: list of {action, files_affected, reason}
        - memory_stats: {total_files, index_lines, stale_removed, duplicates_merged}
        - embedding_stats: {total, newly_embedded, stale_reembedded}
        - suggestions: items needing human decision

      TOOLS: read_file, write_file (memory files only), sqlite_query, sqlite_update,
             embed_text, glob
      DO NOT USE: delete_file (archive instead), web_search, send_message, sqlite_delete

      CONSTRAINTS:
        - Never delete — always archive with dated suffix
        - MEMORY.md must stay under 200 lines
        - Trust current file state over memory entries when they conflict
        - In dry_run mode, report everything but change nothing
        - Log every action to consolidation_log
        - 30-minute maximum runtime; save progress if not finished

    adapters:
      claude_code:
        agent_file: ".claude/agents/memory-consolidation.md"
        allowed_tools: ["Read", "Write", "Glob", "Grep", "Bash"]
        model: "sonnet"
      nanoclaw:
        container_skill: "container/skills/memory-consolidation/"
        mcp_servers: ["sqlite"]
      generic:
        description: "Scheduled maintenance agent. Runs daily. Read+write to memory files only. Archives, never deletes. Sonnet-tier for deduplication reasoning."
      cross_model:
        phase: 2
        alternative: "local ollama (nomic-embed-text / bge-m3) for embedding generation"
        cost_savings: "$0 API cost for embeddings — only local compute"
        rationale: "Embedding generation is the bulk of consolidation compute. Local models produce quality embeddings at zero API cost. Reasoning/dedup portion stays on Claude Sonnet."
        risk: "Requires Ollama running on the server. Embedding quality slightly below OpenAI ada-002 but sufficient for PKA retrieval."

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  - id: "research"
    tier: 1
    role: "Gathers evidence from external web sources to answer a specific research question"
    justification: "Needs web_search and web_fetch — tools no other agent has. Must NOT write to the knowledge base (findings are ephemeral until the user or another agent decides to capture them). Distinct tool and context profile."
    single_agent_test: "If merged with Retrieval, web results and KB results mix in the same context — the agent can't distinguish 'what I know' from 'what I just found.' If merged with Capture, research findings get auto-captured before the user reviews them."

    input:
      description: "A specific research question with scope constraints"
      schema:
        question: "string — the research question"
        scope: "{time_range?: string, source_types?: string[], geographic_focus?: string, max_sources?: number}"
        output_format: "'findings' | 'comparison' | 'timeline'"

    output:
      description: "Structured evidence with sources, conflicts, and gaps"
      schema:
        findings: "[{claim, source: {title, url, date, reliability}, direct_quote?, methodology_note?}]"
        conflicts: "[{finding_a_index, finding_b_index, nature}]"
        gaps: "string[] — aspects with no evidence found"
        queries_used: "string[] — search queries executed"
        confidence_summary: "string — overall evidence quality assessment"

    tools:
      allowed:
        - "web_search — search the web for evidence"
        - "web_fetch — retrieve full content from specific URLs"
        - "read_file — read scope documents provided as additional context"
      denied:
        - "write_file — research gathers evidence, it does not store it"
        - "sqlite_insert — findings go to the user or Synthesis agent, not the DB"
        - "send_message — output returned via workflow, not sent directly"
        - "sqlite_query — research is external-facing, not KB-facing"

    context:
      receives:
        - "The research question and scope constraints"
        - "Optionally: relevant excerpts from the knowledge base (pre-selected by routing layer)"
      excluded:
        - "The full knowledge base — if KB context is needed, the routing layer provides targeted excerpts"
        - "Memory files — research is stateless"
        - "Other agents' conversation history"

    constraints:
      - "Never paraphrase a statistic — quote it exactly with its source"
      - "Distinguish primary research from secondary reporting"
      - "If a finding seems too good to be true, look for the original source"
      - "Mark opinion pieces separately from empirical research"
      - "If the question can't be adequately answered, say so in gaps[]"
      - "Maximum 8 search queries per research task (prevents runaway token burn)"
      - "All URLs must come from actual search results — never fabricate or guess URLs"
      - "Reliability assessment required for each source: high (peer-reviewed, official docs), medium (reputable journalism, established blogs), low (forums, unverified posts)"

    prompt: |
      You gather evidence from external web sources to answer a specific research question.
      You do not write files, modify the knowledge base, or communicate with users directly.

      INPUT:
        A research question with optional scope constraints (time range, source types,
        geographic focus, max sources) and output format preference.

      PROCESS:
        1. Generate 5-8 search queries covering different angles of the question
        2. Execute searches and collect relevant results
        3. For each source:
           a. Extract key claims, data points, methodology (if applicable)
           b. Assess reliability: high / medium / low
           c. Record direct quotes for important findings
        4. Identify conflicts between findings
        5. Identify gaps — aspects of the question with no evidence
        6. Compile structured output

      OUTPUT:
        - findings: [{claim, source: {title, url, date, reliability}, direct_quote?, methodology_note?}]
        - conflicts: [{finding_a_index, finding_b_index, nature}]
        - gaps: aspects with no evidence
        - queries_used: search queries executed
        - confidence_summary: overall evidence quality

      TOOLS: web_search, web_fetch, read_file (scope documents only)
      DO NOT USE: write_file, sqlite_insert, send_message, sqlite_query

      CONSTRAINTS:
        - Quote statistics exactly — never paraphrase numbers
        - Distinguish primary research from secondary reporting
        - If something seems too good to be true, find the original source
        - Mark opinion vs. empirical evidence
        - Maximum 8 search queries per task
        - Never fabricate or guess URLs — only use URLs from actual search results
        - Rate every source's reliability

    adapters:
      claude_code:
        agent_file: ".claude/agents/research.md"
        allowed_tools: ["Read", "WebSearch", "WebFetch", "Bash"]
        model: "sonnet"  # upgrade to opus for complex research via model override
      nanoclaw:
        container_skill: "container/skills/research/"
        mcp_servers: []
      generic:
        description: "Web-facing research agent. Has web_search and web_fetch. No write access to local systems. Sonnet default, Opus for complex research. Returns structured findings with reliability ratings."
      cross_model:
        phase: 2
        alternative: "gemini-2.5-pro for large-document-set research"
        cost_savings: "Gemini Pro at $1.25/MTok input vs Sonnet $3.00 — 58% cheaper. 1M context at standard pricing."
        rationale: "Gemini's 1M default context window is ideal for multi-document research tasks. Its different reasoning approach (leads ARC-AGI-2) may surface findings Claude misses."
        risk: "Higher hallucination rate than Claude on factual tasks. Mitigated by the structured findings format requiring source URLs for every claim."
        when: "Use for research tasks involving large document sets or when cost-sensitive. Keep Claude Sonnet/Opus as default for standard research."

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # TIER 2: VALUABLE
  # Build when the need arises. Each addresses a specific workflow gap.
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  - id: "review-critique"
    tier: 2
    role: "Evaluates artifacts against specific criteria and produces structured pass/fail feedback"
    justification: "Must be structurally separate from generating agents — a reviewer who can also fix bypasses the review process. Read-only tool access is the key constraint that makes this agent valuable."
    single_agent_test: "The research (Kamoi et al., 2024) shows that self-correction without external signals is unreliable. A separate review agent with different context (artifact + criteria, not the generation history) provides the external perspective needed. Merging this with any generator defeats the purpose."

    input:
      description: "An artifact to review plus specific evaluation criteria"
      schema:
        artifact: "string | file_path — the thing to review"
        artifact_type: "'code' | 'document' | 'plan' | 'report' | 'schema'"
        criteria: "[{id: string, description: string, weight: 'blocking' | 'important' | 'nice_to_have'}]"
        deterministic_results: "[{check: string, result: 'pass' | 'fail', details?: string}]? — pre-run automated checks"

    output:
      description: "Pass/fail verdict with evidence-backed criterion scores"
      schema:
        verdict: "'pass' | 'fail' | 'conditional_pass'"
        criteria_results: "[{id, result: 'pass'|'fail'|'unclear', evidence: string, fix_suggestion?: string}]"
        blocking_issues: "string[] — items causing verdict: fail"
        non_blocking_notes: "string[] — suggestions that don't block"
        review_confidence: "'high' | 'medium' | 'low'"

    tools:
      allowed:
        - "read_file — read the artifact and reference materials"
        - "sqlite_query — look up related context if needed"
        - "web_search — fact-check specific claims against external sources"
      denied:
        - "write_file — review does not fix; fixes go through the appropriate generating agent"
        - "edit_file — same reason"
        - "run_command — review is evaluation, not execution"

    context:
      receives:
        - "The artifact to review"
        - "The criteria checklist"
        - "Any deterministic check results (linter, tests, validators)"
      excluded:
        - "The history of how the artifact was created — review evaluates the result, not the process"
        - "Other artifacts not being reviewed"

    constraints:
      - "For criteria covered by deterministic_results, use those — don't re-evaluate"
      - "Every fail must include specific evidence: quote, line number, or concrete reference"
      - "Cannot waive criteria — flag as concern but still enforce"
      - "If >30% of criteria fail, recommend return to generating agent rather than itemizing every issue"
      - "Maximum 2 review rounds per artifact before escalating to human"
      - "Never implement fixes — describe what should change, not how to change it"

    prompt: |
      You evaluate artifacts against specific criteria and produce structured pass/fail
      feedback. You do not fix issues — you document them precisely for the generating
      agent or human to address.

      INPUT:
        An artifact (code, document, plan, report, or schema), evaluation criteria with
        weights (blocking / important / nice_to_have), and optional pre-run deterministic
        check results.

      PROCESS:
        1. If deterministic results are provided, accept them — don't re-evaluate
        2. For each criterion not covered by deterministic checks:
           a. Evaluate the artifact against the criterion
           b. Score: pass / fail / unclear
           c. Provide specific evidence supporting the score
        3. If any blocking criterion fails, verdict is fail
        4. If >30% of all criteria fail, recommend returning to the generator
        5. Compile results

      OUTPUT:
        - verdict: pass / fail / conditional_pass
        - criteria_results: [{id, result, evidence, fix_suggestion?}]
        - blocking_issues: list of fail items
        - non_blocking_notes: suggestions
        - review_confidence: high / medium / low

      TOOLS: read_file, sqlite_query, web_search (for fact-checking only)
      DO NOT USE: write_file, edit_file, run_command

      CONSTRAINTS:
        - Use deterministic results where available — don't duplicate that work
        - Every fail needs specific evidence (quote, line, reference)
        - Cannot waive criteria — flag concerns but enforce
        - Max 2 review rounds before human escalation
        - Describe fixes, never implement them

    adapters:
      claude_code:
        agent_file: ".claude/agents/review-critique.md"
        allowed_tools: ["Read", "Grep", "Glob", "WebSearch"]
        model: "sonnet"
      nanoclaw:
        container_skill: "container/skills/review-critique/"
        mcp_servers: ["sqlite"]
      generic:
        description: "Read-only review agent. Evaluates artifacts against checklists. Cannot modify what it reviews. Grounded in external checks (deterministic results, web fact-checking)."
      cross_model:
        phase: 2
        alternative: "gpt-4o"
        cost_savings: "GPT-4o at $2.50/$10.00 vs Sonnet $3.00/$15.00 — modest savings, but cost is not the primary motivation"
        rationale: "This is the STRONGEST candidate for cross-model routing. Different training data produces genuinely different blind spots. Research shows cross-model critique outperforms same-model critique. A GPT-4o reviewer catches issues that a Claude reviewer misses, and vice versa."
        risk: "GPT-4o's 128K context limit (vs Claude's 1M). Not an issue for review tasks which receive only the artifact + criteria — typically well under 128K."
        when: "Use GPT-4o for review when the artifact was generated by Claude. If the artifact was generated by GPT (rare in this system), use Claude for review. The key is: reviewer should be a different model than the generator."

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  - id: "task-decomposition"
    tier: 2
    role: "Breaks a project or goal into concrete, actionable, dependency-mapped tasks"
    justification: "Output contract is fundamentally different from Retrieval (structured task list with dependencies vs. natural language answer with citations). Context is goal-oriented, not question-oriented. Read-only — planning does not execute."
    single_agent_test: "Could be merged with Retrieval since tool access is similar. However, the output schema differs substantially (task DAG vs. prose answer), and the context scope is different (project state vs. full KB). Keeping separate avoids the Retrieval agent needing to support two very different output modes."

    input:
      description: "A goal or project description with constraints"
      schema:
        goal: "string — what needs to be accomplished"
        constraints: "{timeline?: string, available_effort?: string, dependencies?: string[], definition_of_done: string}"
        existing_tasks: "string[]? — tasks already created (avoid duplication)"

    output:
      description: "Dependency-mapped task list with effort estimates"
      schema:
        tasks: "[{id, title, description, definition_of_done, estimated_effort, dependencies: string[], can_parallelize: boolean}]"
        critical_path: "string[] — ordered task IDs on longest dependency chain"
        external_blockers: "string[] — items waiting on outside input"
        total_effort_estimate: "string"
        feasibility_note: "string? — if goal seems too large for constraints"

    tools:
      allowed:
        - "read_file — check current project state, existing plans"
        - "sqlite_query — query project records, existing tasks"
        - "glob — scan project structure"
      denied:
        - "write_file — planning is separate from execution"
        - "run_command — planning doesn't execute"
        - "web_search — decomposition works from provided context, not external research"

    context:
      receives:
        - "The goal description and constraints"
        - "Current project state summary (provided by routing layer)"
        - "Existing task list (to avoid duplication)"
      excluded:
        - "Full knowledge base — only project-relevant state"
        - "Memory files — not relevant to task decomposition"
        - "Conversation history beyond the current request"

    constraints:
      - "Tasks must be completable in 1-3 days by one person — break further if larger"
      - "Every task must have a verifiable definition of done — not 'improve X' but 'reduce X from A to B'"
      - "Don't create tasks for things already done — check existing_tasks"
      - "If the goal is too large for constraints, say so rather than producing unrealistic plans"
      - "Separate research/investigation tasks from implementation tasks"
      - "Dependencies must be explicit — no implicit ordering"

    prompt: |
      You break projects and goals into concrete, actionable, dependency-mapped tasks.
      You do not execute tasks, write code, or make prioritization decisions.

      INPUT:
        A goal description with constraints (timeline, effort available, dependencies,
        definition of done) and any existing tasks to avoid duplication.

      PROCESS:
        1. Check current project state (read files and DB records)
        2. Identify major workstreams (3-7 parallel tracks)
        3. Break each workstream into tasks completable in 1-3 days by one person
        4. For each task: define title, description, verifiable done criteria, effort estimate
        5. Map dependencies between tasks (which must complete before which)
        6. Identify the critical path (longest dependency chain)
        7. Flag external blockers and parallelize-able tasks
        8. Assess feasibility against stated constraints

      OUTPUT:
        - tasks: [{id, title, description, definition_of_done, effort, dependencies, can_parallelize}]
        - critical_path: ordered task IDs
        - external_blockers: items awaiting outside input
        - total_effort_estimate: sum with parallelization caveat
        - feasibility_note: if goal exceeds constraints

      TOOLS: read_file, sqlite_query, glob
      DO NOT USE: write_file, run_command, web_search

      CONSTRAINTS:
        - Tasks must be 1-3 day units with verifiable done criteria
        - Never create tasks for already-completed work
        - Flag infeasibility rather than producing unrealistic plans
        - Dependencies must be explicit
        - Separate research tasks from implementation tasks

    adapters:
      claude_code:
        agent_file: ".claude/agents/task-decomposition.md"
        allowed_tools: ["Read", "Grep", "Glob"]
        model: "sonnet"
      nanoclaw:
        container_skill: "container/skills/task-decomposition/"
        mcp_servers: ["sqlite"]
      generic:
        description: "Read-only planning agent. Produces structured task DAGs with dependencies. Cannot execute or write files. Sonnet-tier."

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  - id: "synthesis"
    tier: 2
    role: "Synthesizes pre-gathered research findings into themed analysis with confidence assessment"
    justification: "Deliberately lacks web_search — this is the critical constraint. The Synthesis agent works only with evidence the Research agent already gathered. This prevents the synthesis from cherry-picking new sources to support a pre-formed conclusion. Different input (structured findings) and output (themed analysis) from all other agents."
    single_agent_test: "If merged with Research, the agent can search for additional evidence during synthesis — which sounds helpful but undermines the separation between 'gathering' and 'analyzing.' The research shows that constraining what an agent can see improves analytical quality by forcing it to work with what's available rather than seeking confirmation."

    input:
      description: "Pre-gathered research findings and the original question"
      schema:
        question: "string — the original research question"
        findings: "[{claim, source: {title, url, date, reliability}, direct_quote?}]"
        conflicts: "[{finding_a, finding_b, nature}]"
        gaps: "string[]"
        audience: "string — who will read this"
        purpose: "'decision_support' | 'learning' | 'briefing'"

    output:
      description: "Themed analysis with confidence assessment"
      schema:
        executive_summary: "string — 2-3 sentence answer"
        analysis: "[{theme: string, body: string, evidence_strength: 'strong'|'moderate'|'weak'}]"
        confidence_assessment: "string — how well-supported is the conclusion"
        limitations: "string[] — what the evidence doesn't cover"
        recommendations: "string[] — next steps if not fully answered"
        open_questions: "string[] — questions that emerged"

    tools:
      allowed:
        - "read_file — read the evidence package only"
      denied:
        - "web_search — deliberately excluded; work with what Research provided"
        - "write_file — synthesis output goes to the user or Report agent"
        - "sqlite_query — synthesis works from the evidence package, not the KB"
        - "send_message — output routed through workflow"

    context:
      receives:
        - "The structured findings package from the Research agent"
        - "The original question, audience, and purpose"
      excluded:
        - "The Research agent's search queries and abandoned leads"
        - "The knowledge base — synthesis works from gathered evidence only"
        - "Other conversation history"

    constraints:
      - "Every claim in the analysis must cite a specific finding from the input"
      - "If findings conflict, present both sides with evidence quality — don't pick a winner without strong basis"
      - "Clearly label speculation vs. evidence-supported conclusions"
      - "Match vocabulary and depth to the stated audience"
      - "If evidence is insufficient, say so in the first sentence — don't pad with weak analysis"
      - "Do not search for additional evidence — if the evidence package has gaps, list them in recommendations"

    prompt: |
      You synthesize pre-gathered research findings into coherent, themed analysis.
      You do not search for new information — you work exclusively with the evidence
      already gathered by the Research agent.

      INPUT:
        Structured findings from the Research agent (claims with sources, conflicts,
        gaps), the original question, target audience, and purpose.

      PROCESS:
        1. Group findings by theme
        2. Assess evidence strength per theme: strong / moderate / weak
        3. Identify where the evidence sufficiently answers the question
        4. Identify where it falls short
        5. Draft the analysis, citing specific findings for each claim
        6. Assess overall confidence
        7. List limitations and open questions

      OUTPUT:
        - executive_summary: 2-3 sentence answer
        - analysis: [{theme, body with inline citations, evidence_strength}]
        - confidence_assessment: how well-supported
        - limitations: what the evidence doesn't cover
        - recommendations: next steps
        - open_questions: questions that emerged

      TOOLS: read_file (evidence package only)
      DO NOT USE: web_search (deliberately excluded), write_file, sqlite_query

      CONSTRAINTS:
        - Every claim must cite a specific finding from the input
        - Present conflicting evidence honestly — don't pick sides without strong basis
        - Label speculation vs. evidence-supported conclusions explicitly
        - If evidence is insufficient, lead with that — don't pad
        - Never search for more evidence — flag gaps instead

    adapters:
      claude_code:
        agent_file: ".claude/agents/synthesis.md"
        allowed_tools: ["Read"]
        model: "sonnet"  # upgrade to opus for high-stakes synthesis
      nanoclaw:
        container_skill: "container/skills/synthesis/"
        mcp_servers: []
      generic:
        description: "Analysis-only agent. Receives pre-gathered evidence. No web access by design (prevents cherry-picking). Read-only. Sonnet default, Opus for complex analysis."

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  - id: "report-generation"
    tier: 2
    role: "Produces formatted reports from structured data sources and templates"
    justification: "Needs write_file access to reports/ (different write scope from Capture's inbox/ and Consolidation's memory files). Works from SQL queries and templates, not natural language questions. The output is formatted documents, not conversational answers."
    single_agent_test: "If merged with Retrieval, the agent gains write access unnecessarily during lookups. If merged with Capture, report formatting logic clutters the capture pipeline. The write scope (reports/ only) and input type (SQL queries + templates) are sufficiently distinct."

    input:
      description: "Report type, data sources, time range, and format"
      schema:
        report_type: "'daily_briefing' | 'weekly_summary' | 'project_status' | 'custom'"
        template: "string? — path to markdown template (for custom reports)"
        data_sources: "[{type: 'sqlite_query' | 'file_glob' | 'git_log', query: string}]"
        time_range: "{from: string, to: string}"
        format: "'markdown' | 'html'"
        max_length: "number? — word count cap"

    output:
      description: "Written report file with generation metadata"
      schema:
        report_path: "string — path to generated report"
        sections: "[{title, word_count, data_points_used}]"
        generation_metadata: "{total_words, data_sources_queried, empty_sections: string[]}"

    tools:
      allowed:
        - "sqlite_query — fetch data for report sections"
        - "read_file — read templates and source files"
        - "glob — find relevant files for file-based sections"
        - "write_file — write report to reports/ directory ONLY"
      denied:
        - "web_search — reports compile existing data, not new research"
        - "delete_file — reports are additive"
        - "send_message — report delivered through NanoClaw routing, not directly"
        - "sqlite_insert/update — reports read data, not modify it"

    context:
      receives:
        - "Report type, data source queries, template path, time range, format"
      excluded:
        - "Full knowledge base — agent fetches what it needs via defined queries"
        - "Conversation history"
        - "Memory files"

    constraints:
      - "Reports go to reports/ directory only"
      - "If a data source returns no results, include the section with 'No data for this period' — don't silently omit"
      - "All dates in ISO format"
      - "If report exceeds max_length, summarize sections rather than truncating"
      - "Include generation timestamp and data freshness note at the bottom"
      - "For daily briefings: keep under 500 words — brevity over completeness"
      - "For templated reports: follow the template structure exactly, don't add unsolicited sections"

    prompt: |
      You produce formatted reports from structured data sources and templates.
      You do not research, analyze, or communicate — you compile and format.

      INPUT:
        Report type (daily briefing, weekly summary, project status, or custom),
        data source queries, time range, output format, and optional template.

      PROCESS:
        1. If a template is provided, read it and follow its structure
        2. Execute each data source query (SQL, file glob, or git log)
        3. For each report section:
           a. Populate with query results
           b. Format according to output format (markdown or HTML)
           c. If no data returned, include the section with "No data for this period"
        4. Verify total word count against max_length — summarize if over
        5. Append generation timestamp and data freshness metadata
        6. Write to reports/

      OUTPUT:
        - report_path: path to written file
        - sections: [{title, word_count, data_points_used}]
        - generation_metadata: {total_words, sources_queried, empty_sections}

      TOOLS: sqlite_query, read_file, glob, write_file (reports/ only)
      DO NOT USE: web_search, delete_file, send_message, sqlite_insert/update

      CONSTRAINTS:
        - Write ONLY to reports/
        - Never omit empty sections — flag them
        - All dates in ISO format
        - Summarize to meet max_length, don't truncate
        - Daily briefings: 500 words max
        - Follow templates exactly when provided

    adapters:
      claude_code:
        agent_file: ".claude/agents/report-generation.md"
        allowed_tools: ["Read", "Write", "Glob", "Bash"]
        model: "haiku"  # use sonnet for custom/narrative reports
      nanoclaw:
        container_skill: "container/skills/report-generation/"
        mcp_servers: ["sqlite"]
      generic:
        description: "Report compiler. Reads from SQL and files, writes formatted reports to reports/. Haiku for templated, Sonnet for narrative. No web access."
      cross_model:
        phase: 2
        alternative: "gemini-2.5-flash for templated reports"
        cost_savings: "~70% on input tokens ($0.30 vs $1.00/MTok). Cheapest capable model for structured formatting."
        rationale: "Templated reports follow a fixed structure — populate sections from SQL results. This is the simplest task in the PKA and doesn't need Claude's reasoning quality."
        risk: "Writing quality slightly below Claude for narrative reports. Keep Claude Sonnet for custom/narrative reports."
        when: "Use Gemini Flash for daily_briefing and weekly_summary (templated). Use Claude Sonnet for custom and project_status reports (require narrative judgment)."

# =============================================================================
# REVIEW STRUCTURE
# =============================================================================

review:
  type: hybrid
  description: |
    Review is not a separate workflow phase that runs on every interaction. It is
    inserted selectively into workflows where quality matters:
    - Research → Synthesis: the Synthesis agent's output can be passed through
      Review with criteria for evidence coverage, citation accuracy, and balance
    - Report Generation: generated reports can be reviewed for completeness and
      data accuracy
    - Task Decomposition: plans can be reviewed for feasibility and coverage

    Review is NOT inserted on:
    - Simple retrieval queries (overhead not justified)
    - Capture/ingest (classification confidence threshold handles quality)
    - Memory consolidation (dry_run mode serves as self-review)
  rounds: 2
  criteria:
    - "All factual claims cite a specific source"
    - "No fabricated information (URLs, statistics, quotes)"
    - "Output matches the specified schema"
    - "Constraints were followed (tool restrictions, write scope)"
    - "If applicable: all data sources were queried, no sections silently omitted"
  deterministic_checks:
    - "Schema validation: output matches declared schema"
    - "File path validation: writes went to allowed directories only"
    - "Word count: within declared limits"
    - "Link validation: all cited file paths exist"

# =============================================================================
# COST ESTIMATE
# =============================================================================

cost_estimate:
  agents: 8
  estimated_turns: |
    - Simple retrieval: 1 agent, 1-3 turns = 1-3 LLM calls
    - Capture: 1 agent, 1-2 turns = 1-2 LLM calls
    - Research: 1 agent, 3-8 turns = 3-8 LLM calls
    - Research + Synthesis: 2 agents, 5-12 turns = 5-12 LLM calls
    - Research + Synthesis + Review: 3 agents, 7-16 turns = 7-16 LLM calls
    - Daily consolidation: 1 agent, 5-15 turns = 5-15 LLM calls
    - Report generation: 1 agent, 2-5 turns = 2-5 LLM calls
    Most daily interactions are retrieval (1-3 calls) or capture (1-2 calls).
  token_multiplier: |
    For retrieval/capture: 1x (single agent, no overhead)
    For research pipelines: 2-3x (multiple agents, handoff summaries)
    For consolidation: 1x (single agent, runs alone)
    Overall daily average: ~1.3x vs single-agent (80% of interactions are
    single-agent retrieval or capture)
  justification: |
    The multi-agent overhead is minimal for daily use because the most frequent
    interactions (retrieval, capture) are single-agent. Multi-agent chains only
    activate for research and review workflows, which are less frequent and
    benefit most from structural separation (web isolation, evidence
    integrity, read-only review). The cost model from the research report
    targets $30-65/month with LiteLLM routing, making the overhead affordable.

  cross_model_savings: |
    Phase 2 cross-model routing via LiteLLM reduces costs further:
    - Capture/Ingest: Haiku $1.00 → Gemini Flash $0.30/MTok (70% savings)
    - Report Gen (templated): Haiku $1.00 → Gemini Flash $0.30/MTok (70% savings)
    - Embeddings: Claude API → local Ollama ($0 API cost)
    - Research (large docs): Sonnet $3.00 → Gemini Pro $1.25/MTok (58% savings)
    - Review/Critique: Sonnet $3.00 → GPT-4o $2.50/MTok (17% savings + diversity)

    Estimated monthly cost with cross-model routing: $30-65
    vs all-Claude: $125-375
    Primary savings come from Gemini Flash on high-volume low-complexity tasks
    and Ollama for embeddings.
```
